codeReview.summary: The cross-config-cascade feature implements integration tests verifying that a five-layer config cascade is respected by ExecutionController, with max_iterations loaded through the config cascade. Implementation includes ConfigLoader fixes for nested env var parsing (SWELL_EXECUTION_MAX_ITERATIONS → execution.max_iterations), record_entries_recursive for audit trail of nested keys, and 8 test cases covering the cascade, overrides, and ExecutionController integration. The tests pass and clippy is clean.

issues.description: Copy-paste error in test_env_var_overrides_file_layers: Layer 3 is set to {"max_iterations": 30} but then immediately overwritten when the same file path .swell/settings.json is created again with {"max_iterations": 40}. The test still passes because env var ultimately wins, but the setup is confusing and doesn't match the intended 4-file-layer cascade of 10/20/30/40 + env=100. The actual values at each layer are 10/20/40/40 instead of 10/20/30/40.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Handoff references commit 4671ca4 (clippy fixes to cross_memory_integration.rs) but the cross_config_cascade.rs test file was actually added in commit 51a86b1 (workspace clean clippy pass). The feature implementation (test file + ConfigLoader fixes) is in 51a86b1, not 4671ca4 as stated in handoff. This is a minor discrepancy — the handoff captures the work correctly but references the wrong commit ID.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.commitId=4671ca4 but git log shows cross_config_cascade.rs added in 51a86b1. The ConfigLoader fixes (load_env_overrides underscore parsing, record_entries_recursive) are also in 51a86b1.

summary: Feature cross-config-cascade passes scrutiny. Implementation includes 7 integration tests for five-layer config cascade with max_iterations, plus 1 unit test in swell-core for nested env var override. ConfigLoader was fixed to properly parse SWELL_EXECUTION_MAX_ITERATIONS → execution.max_iterations (only first underscore converted) and record nested keys in audit trail via record_entries_recursive. Tests pass and clippy is clean. One non-blocking code issue: copy-paste error in test setup where Layer 3 file is overwritten with Layer 4's value, but this doesn't affect test correctness since env var still wins.
